title: Benefits Specialist
basic: 1-3 years of experience in a US-based, employee-facing benefits operations role.
Proficiency with Workday, specifically the Benefits and Time Off modules.
Strong understanding of employee benefits programs with excellent written and verbal English communication skills to explain complex information.
Experience with benefits administration tasks including enrollments, invoicing, and maintaining data accuracy for vendors.
Strong problem-solving, analytical, and organizational skills with a focus on process improvement.
Ability to handle confidential and sensitive data with discretion and judgment.
bonus: Experience with AMER benefit portals (e.g., Forma, HMSA, Risco).
Experience working in a fast-paced, growing environment, such as a technology company or large-scale organization.
Experience with benefits operations and programs in EMEA and APAC regions.
Experience applying company policies and practices to make appropriate recommendations.

title: Benefits Specialist
basic: 1-3 years of experience in an employee-facing benefits operations role with EMEA/APAC insurance and benefits experience
Experience using Workday, specifically the Benefits and Time Off modules
Strong understanding of employee benefits programs and ability to explain complex information clearly and empathetically
Experience with accurate benefits data processing, reporting, and analysis
Strong problem-solving, cross-team collaboration, and process improvement skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Detail-oriented, analytical, adaptable, and organized with strong operational execution skills
Discretion and judgment in handling confidential and sensitive data
bonus: Experience working with benefits vendors and brokers/consultants
Experience working in a fast-paced, growing environment, such as a technology company or large-scale organization
Experience utilizing company policies and practices to determine appropriate recommendations
Experience with benefits operations and programs in the U.S., Canada, and LATAM

title: Business Recruiter (Fixed Term Contract)
basic: 5+ years of full-cycle Business and/or General Recruiting experience in a high-growth, in-house environment
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Proven ability to attract passive senior candidates and build diverse pipelines
Data-driven approach to problem-solving and process improvement
bonus: Passion for team building and delivering an exceptional candidate experience
Growth mindset with flexibility to adapt to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment
Interest in working in a cross-functional, fluid environment with an ability to learn quickly

title: Go-To-Market Recruiter (Fixed Term Contract)
basic: 4+ years of full-cycle, in-house recruiting experience in a high-growth environment
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to build relationships with and influence senior leaders and stakeholders
Experience with strategic sourcing and engaging top talent
Proven ability to use data and metrics to inform and refine recruiting strategies
Flexibility to adapt to changing priorities and tasks
bonus: Proficiency with a modern recruiting tech stack, including ATS (e.g., Greenhouse, Lever), sourcing/CRM tools (e.g., Gem, LinkedIn Recruiter), analytics platforms, and collaboration tools (e.g., G Suite, Slack)

title: Go-To-Market Recruiter (Fixed Term Contract)
basic: 4+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience in a high-growth environment, including in-house experience.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with business proficiency in English.
Experience building relationships with and influencing senior leaders.
Demonstrated ability to strategically source, engage, and build relationships with top talent.
Proven ability to use data insights to analyze trends and refine recruiting processes.
Flexibility to adapt and manage shifting priorities in a dynamic environment.
bonus: Proficiency in French, German, or Spanish.

title: Go-To-Market Recruiter (Fixed Term Contract)
basic: 4+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience in a fast-paced, high-growth environment, including in-house experience
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with business proficiency in English
Strong ability to build relationships with and influence senior leaders
Strong drive to identify, engage, and foster relationships with top talent
Proven ability to use data insights to identify trends and refine recruiting processes
Flexibility and adaptability to shift tasks and priorities in a changing environment

title: Go-To-Market Recruiter (Fixed Term Contract)
basic: 4+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience in a fast-paced, high-growth environment, including in-house experience.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to build relationships with and influence senior leaders.
Strong drive to identify, engage, and build relationships with top talent.
Proven ability to use data insights to analyze trends and improve recruiting processes.
Flexibility and adaptability in a dynamic environment with shifting priorities.
bonus: Proficiency with a modern recruiting tech stack, including ATS (e.g., Greenhouse, Lever), CRM/sourcing tools (e.g., Gem, LinkedIn Recruiter), analytics platforms, and collaboration tools (e.g., G Suite, Slack).
Ability to quickly adopt new recruiting technologies and optimize workflows for scale.

title: Payroll Coordinator
basic: Understanding of payroll regulations in Singapore and India
Proficiency in English
Proficiency with payroll software and systems
Intermediate proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets for managing data, formulas, and functions
Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills and an aptitude for working with data (e.g., timesheets, leave accruals, government surveys)
bonus: Experience with ADP, Workday, or similar payroll platforms

title: Payroll Analyst
basic: 5+ years of end-to-end US payroll processing experience in a fast-paced public company environment.
Expertise in Workday Payroll, including processing, configuration, issue resolution, and reporting.
In-depth knowledge of federal and multi-state payroll tax regulations and compliance.
Experience with month-end accounting processes, including journal entries and account reconciliation, using systems like NetSuite.
Strong communication and customer service skills for interacting with employees and stakeholders at all levels.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize effectively, and meet deadlines in a high-volume setting.
High attention to detail with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
bonus: Certified Payroll Professional (CPP) designation.
Curiosity and willingness to learn and leverage AI tools and emerging technologies.

title: Talent Acquisition Manager
basic: 8+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience, including 5+ years leading technical recruiting teams in-house at a tech company
Experience hiring in Poland and other EU countries
Experience working with international teams and offices
Demonstrated ability to lead, motivate, and coach recruiting teams
Proactive problem-solving skills with the ability to develop creative hiring approaches
Strong stakeholder management skills with experience driving organizational change and fostering a culture of shared recruiting accountability
A data-driven approach to decision-making to improve operational effectiveness
bonus: Curiosity and willingness to learn and leverage AI tools and emerging technologies to enhance productivity

title: Talent Acquisition Partner
basic: 8+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience, preferably in-house for technical teams at a tech company.
Experience hiring in Poland and other EU countries.
Experience working with international teams, global offices, and senior leadership.
Ability to design and execute recruiting strategies, including developing candidate assessment tools.
Experience with proactive candidate sourcing and building relationships with passive candidates.
Demonstrated commitment to providing an excellent candidate experience.
Data-driven and analytical approach to recruiting, with a sense of urgency.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and creating structure in uncertain environments.
bonus: Experience launching a new office or being an early recruiter in a new region.
Curiosity and willingness to learn and leverage AI tools and emerging technologies.

title: Recruiter, Early Career Talent
basic: 2+ years of recruiting/HR experience, with at least 2 years specifically in university recruiting
Experience with high-volume, mid to large-scale early career recruiting programs
Experience using an Applicant Tracking System (ATS) to manage candidate pipelines
Experience partnering with sourcers to develop effective sourcing strategies
Experience using data and metrics to generate insights and guide recruiting strategy
Experience mentoring teammates
Demonstrated ability to manage top talent and provide an excellent candidate experience
Ability to solve complex recruiting problems with creativity and innovation
Willingness and ability to travel up to 25% for recruiting events across the U.S. and Canada
Possesses a startup mentality and is open to innovative thinking

title: Senior G&A Recruiter
basic: 4+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience with a focus on G&A roles (e.g., Finance, Accounting, People, Marketing) in a fast-paced environment
Proven ability to recruit for senior and/or niche positions
Experience analyzing recruiting metrics to drive decision-making and process improvements
Experience working cross-functionally with HR teams such as Compensation, People Operations, and Recruiting Operations
Comprehensive knowledge of HR practices and employment laws
BS in Human Resources Management or a relevant degree
Willingness to work in the San Francisco office two days per week (Wednesday and Thursday)
bonus: Experience with Greenhouse ATS
Experience working in a high-growth startup environment
Knowledge of employment laws in both the US and Canada

title: Senior Technical Recruiter, AI
basic: Proven experience recruiting for technical roles (e.g., software engineering, AI, data, product) in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
Strong sourcing skills with familiarity in modern recruiting tools and platforms.
Excellent communication, relationship-building, and stakeholder management abilities, including with executive leadership (CEO/CTO).
Data-driven mindset with the ability to use recruiting metrics to drive process improvements.
Ability to manage multiple searches and priorities simultaneously.
Commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion in all recruiting practices.
bonus: Strong knowledge of compensation strategy and market analysis for highly technical roles, particularly for AI engineering talent.
Executive hiring experience, including partnering with senior leadership on senior-level or niche searches.
Experience driving employer branding and talent marketing initiatives to attract top engineering talent.
Proven success recruiting across different geographies (e.g., US, Canada, LATAM).
